REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT PETER ISKANDAR, LEE B. DO LLC. MOTION AND NOTICE OF HEARING ON MOTION FOR CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 425.16 SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE (ANTI-SLAPP) COMPLAINT IS DENIED IN PART AND GRANTED IN PART. PURSUANT TO BARAL V. SCHNITT (AUGUST 1, 2016) 1 CAL.5TH 376 EACH CLAIM (REGARDLESS OF ITS INCLUSION INTO ONE CAUSE OF ACTION WITH OTHER CLAIMS) IS TO BE EVALUATED UNDER THE FIRST PRONG OF CCP 425.16. MOVING PARTY FAILED TO SUSTAIN ITS BURDEN RE: IIED AND UCL TO BASED ON THE CLAIMS LISTED UNDER A-C; BREACH OF QUIET ENJOYMENT; SFRO 37.10B; NEGLIGENCE BASED ON INVASION OF PRIVACY AND IIED. THEREFORE, MOTION TO STRIKE AS TO THESE CLAIMS IS DENIED. MOVING PARTY SUSTAINED ITS BURDEN UNDER THE FIRST PRONG AS CLAIMS FOR WRONGFUL USE OF FIRST ADMINISTRATIVE PROCEEDING; WRONGFUL USE OF SECOND ADMINISTRATIVE PROCEEDING; IIED AND UCL TO BASED ON THE CLAIMS LISTED UNDER D-E; AND NEGLIGENCE BASED ON WRONGFUL USE OF ADMINISTRATIVE PROCEEDINGS AND ELDER ABUSE. OPPOSING PARTY FAILED TO SHOW PROBABILITY OF PREVAILING ON THESE CLAIMS WITH COMPETENT ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE SUPPORTING EACH ELEMENT OF EACH OF THESE CLAIMS. AS TO THE CAUSE OF ACTION TO ATTORNEY'S FEES, THE CCP 425.16 MOTION IS DENIED. IRRESPECTIVE OF THE LABEL "EIGHTS CAUSE OF ACTION" THIS IS MERELY A PRAYER NOT A CAUSE OF ACTION OR A CLAIM, SUBJECT TO CCP 425.16. (AS FULLY REPORTED IN THE REPORTER'S NOTES) ORDER IS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS)JUDGE: RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: NOEL CARTER DEGNAN, CSR #6921, (415-362-4346), REPORTED (501).

TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS TO MATERIAL FACTS 1, 6 AND 7. ADDITIONALLY MOVING PARTY FAILED TO ESTABLISH THAT IT IS ENTITLED TO A JUDGMENT AS A MATTER OF LAW. MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Marc Libarle (415-28-2400) for Plaintiff/ opposing party William Dawson.

Tamara Willat (415-272-1753), CSR #4609 Court reporter.

The Court anounces the tentative ruling. Hearing is held. Argued re: shifting burden, witness declarations, case citations and triable issues of fact. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT PETER ISKANDAR and LEE B. DO LLC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication is DENIED. Triable issue of fact as to material facts 1, 6 and 7. Additionally moving party failed to establish that it is entitled to a judgment as a matter of law. Prevailing party to submit order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: A. Pascual, Tamara Willat (415-272-1753), CSR #4609 Court reporter. (D501)

Motion And Notice Of Hearing On Motion For Code Of Civil Procedure Section 425.16 Special Motion To Strike (Anti-Slapp) Complaint
Off Calendar Nov-10-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
David Wasserman, Esq. (415-567-9600) for Defendant/moving party PETER ISKANDAR, LEE B. DO LLC.
No other Appearance.
Court reporter: Noel Carter Degnan, CSR #6921, (415-362-4346), Reported (501).

No Argument. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT PETER ISKANDAR, LEE B. DO LLC. Motion And Notice Of Hearing On Motion For Code Of Civil Procedure Section 425.16 Special Motion To Strike (Anti-Slapp) Complaint
is DENIED in part and GRANTED in part. Pursuant to Baral v. Schnitt (August 1, 2016) 1 Cal.5th 376 each claim (regardless of its inclusion into one cause of action with other claims) is to be evaluated under the first prong of CCP 425.16.
Moving party failed to sustain its burden re: IIED and UCL to based on the claims listed under a-c; Breach of Quiet Enjoyment; SFRO 37.10B; negligence based on invasion of privacy and IIED. Therefore, Motion to Strike as to these claims is DENIED.
Moving party sustained its burden under the first prong as claims for wrongful use of first administrative proceeding; wrongful use of second administrative proceeding; IIED and UCL to based on the claims listed under d-e; and Negligence based on wrongful use of administrative proceedings and elder abuse. Opposing party failed to show probability of prevailing on these claims with competent admissible evidence supporting each element of each of these claims.
As to the cause of action to attorney's fees, the CCP 425.16 motion is denied. Irrespective of the label "Eights cause of action" this is merely a prayer not a cause of action or a claim, subject to CCP 425.16. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) Order is signed in open court. (see scanned order for details)Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court reporter: Noel Carter Degnan, CSR #6921, (415-362-4346), Reported (501).
